subtasks.description: Query Salesforce license inventory: total licensed seat count by license type (User, Platform User, etc.) and current org capacity
Query Salesforce user records: count of active seat assignments by license type, profile assignments, and last login date to identify unused accounts

Reconcile licensed seat counts against actual assignments; calculate unutilized licenses by type; identify inactive or dormant accounts; classify utilization posture (healthy, underutilized, or wasteful); recommend deactivation or license optimization actions

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks: (1) License inventory query (small tier—single-table snapshot query of org settings), (2) User assignment query (mid tier—requires joining user table with profile data and filtering by activity status), (3) Cross-reconciliation (mid tier—compares two data sources, calculates gaps, and generates classification/recommendations). This avoids splitting two independent queries into three by keeping license and assignment queries separate (they have different substrates), then consolidating all analysis and posture classification into one cross_recon step. Cost is minimized by avoiding unnecessary extract or policy_action subtasks (utilization analysis is analytical, not policy compliance).

output.sql: SELECT license_id, product AS license_type, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_licensed_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS assigned_seats, SUM(daily_active_seats) - COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unutilized_seats, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) / NULLIF(SUM(daily_active_seats), 0), 2) AS utilization_percentage FROM consumption_facts WHERE event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY license_id, product ORDER BY total_licensed_seats DESC, utilization_percentage ASC

SELECT publisher, product, license_id, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS assigned_users, MAX(daily_active_seats) AS max_daily_active_seats, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_daily_active_seats, MAX(event_date) AS last_activity_date, MIN(event_date) AS first_activity_date, COUNT(DISTINCT event_date) AS active_days, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_spend, ROUND(AVG(daily_active_seats), 2) AS avg_daily_active_seats, MAX(event_date) AS last_login_date, CASE WHEN MAX(event_date) < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' THEN 'Inactive - 30+ days' WHEN MAX(event_date) < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '14 days' THEN 'Inactive - 14-30 days' WHEN MAX(event_date) < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days' THEN 'Inactive - 7-14 days' ELSE 'Active' END AS activity_status FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher ILIKE '%salesforce%' GROUP BY publisher, product, license_id ORDER BY last_activity_date ASC, total_spend DESC
